data.title: Gecoss Raises Full-Year Earnings Outlook and Hikes Year-End Dividend
data.description:
Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Gecoss Corporation ( (JP:9991) ) has shared an announcement.

Gecoss Corporation revised its full-year consolidated earnings forecast for the year ending March 31, 2026, raising expected net sales from ¥110.0 billion to ¥113.0 billion and boosting projected operating income, ordinary income and profit attributable to owners of parent by double-digit percentages versus the previous forecast, reflecting steady progress in heavy temporary construction projects and ongoing efforts to enhance profitability. In line with stronger earnings expectations and its medium-term dividend policy targeting around a 40% payout ratio and a DOE of at least 2.5%, the company also raised its year-end dividend forecast from ¥34 to ¥40 per share, lifting the projected annual dividend to ¥65 and signaling an increased emphasis on shareholder returns as profits improve versus the prior fiscal year.

The most recent analyst rating on (JP:9991) stock is a Buy with a Yen1811.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Gecoss Corporation stock, see the JP:9991 Stock Forecast page.

More about Gecoss Corporation

Gecoss Corporation, listed on the Tokyo Stock Exchange Prime Market, operates in the heavy temporary construction sector, supplying related construction services and solutions with a focus on profitability improvement and stable shareholder returns.

Average Trading Volume: 46,869

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: Yen51.17B

data.title: Nokia Sets Board Chair Succession, New Director and Capital Distribution Plan Ahead of 2026 AGM
data.description:
Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Nokia ( (GB:0HAF) ) has issued an update.

Nokia’s Board of Directors has published its key proposals for the 2026 Annual General Meeting, setting out changes in board leadership, composition, capital distribution and share issuance authorities. Longtime Chair Sari Baldauf will step down after the AGM, with current board member and CFO Timo Ihamuotila proposed as the new Chair and Thomas Saueressig as Vice Chair, while Meredith Whittaker, President of the Signal Technology Foundation, is nominated as a new board member, keeping the board at ten directors. Board and committee fees are proposed to remain unchanged, with roughly 40% of annual compensation paid in Nokia shares, reinforcing alignment between directors and shareholders. The Board is seeking authorization to distribute up to EUR 0.14 per share in four installments via dividend and/or return of capital, consistent with Nokia’s dividend policy, and to reappoint Deloitte Oy as both financial auditor and sustainability reporting assurer for the 2027 financial year. In addition, the Board requests fresh authorizations to issue and to repurchase up to 550 million shares—less than 10% of Nokia’s share capital—to support capital structure management, potential acquisitions, equity-based incentives and other strategic purposes, decisions that collectively underscore Nokia’s focus on governance continuity, shareholder returns and financial flexibility ahead of its next strategic phase.

More about Nokia

Nokia is a global telecommunications and technology company that develops and supplies network infrastructure, software, and related services to mobile and fixed operators, enterprises and webscale companies worldwide, with a strategic focus on 5G, cloud, and digital infrastructure solutions.

For a thorough assessment of 0HAF stock, go to TipRanks’ Stock Analysis page.

data.title: Liberty Metals Secures 100% Ownership of Brazilian Critical Minerals Portfolio and Rebrands
data.description:
Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

An announcement from Traka Resources Limited ( (AU:LIB) ) is now available.

Liberty Metals has completed the exercise of an option to acquire 100% of a portfolio of Brazilian critical minerals projects, including the Paraíba Hard Rock Rutile Project, the Rio Grande Heavy Mineral Sands Project and the Alcobaça Heavy Mineral Sands & Rare Earths Project, consolidating a multi-asset position in titanium minerals and rare earth elements. Backed by shareholder approval and due diligence, the transaction marks a key growth milestone that expands Liberty’s strategic presence in Brazil’s Tier-1 mining districts, offers clear geological and commercial analogues via proximity to large established resources and recent M&A transactions, and underpins the company’s rebrand to Liberty Metals Limited as it pursues accelerated exploration, further project and corporate opportunities, and value creation for shareholders.

More about Traka Resources Limited

Liberty Metals Ltd (ASX: LIB) is an Australian-listed resources company focused on critical minerals, particularly titanium minerals and rare earth elements, with a growing strategic footprint in Brazil. The company is targeting hard rock rutile, heavy mineral sands and monazite sands projects in established Brazilian mineral provinces, positioning itself within globally recognised Tier-1 mining jurisdictions and adjacent to advanced peer developments in the critical minerals sector.

Current Market Cap: A$32.8M

data.title: Bajaj Mobility Swings to Strong Profit on Restructuring Gain Despite Revenue Slump
data.description:
Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

PIERER Mobility AG ( (CH:BMAG) ) has issued an announcement.

Bajaj Mobility AG reported preliminary unaudited figures for 2025 showing revenue of EUR 1,009 million, down 46% year-on-year due to a restructuring phase, but a dramatic turnaround in profitability driven by a restructuring gain of EUR 1,193 million from completed proceedings at key KTM entities. EBITDA swung from a loss of EUR 481 million to a positive EUR 874 million and EBIT improved from a loss of EUR 1,184 million to EUR 748 million, while net debt was cut by roughly half to EUR 798 million, equity turned positive at EUR 385 million and the equity ratio rose to 24.3%, alongside sharply lower investments and headcount. For 2026, management plans to continue restructuring with a strong focus on the core motorcycle business, fixed-cost reductions, portfolio streamlining and efficiency gains, targeting a clear return to positive cash flow and a significant increase in revenue and unit sales, which, if achieved, would further consolidate the company’s financial recovery and competitive position in the motorcycle market.

The most recent analyst rating on (CH:BMAG) stock is a Hold with a CHF28.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on PIERER Mobility AG stock, see the CH:BMAG Stock Forecast page.

More about PIERER Mobility AG

Bajaj Mobility AG, formerly PIERER Mobility AG, is the holding company of KTM AG, one of Europe’s leading motorcycle manufacturers. The group brings together the KTM, Husqvarna Motorcycles and GASGAS brands, offering high-performance racing bikes, street and offroad motorcycles and travel models, complemented by premium WP components. With a strong emphasis on performance, innovation and cutting‑edge technology, and a broad international presence, the company positions itself as a benchmark player in the global motorcycle industry.

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Current Market Cap: CHF509M

data.title: Nokia Delivers In-Line 2025 Results, Tightens Focus on AI-Era Network Growth
data.description:
Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Nokia ( (GB:0HAF) ) has shared an announcement.

Nokia reported a broadly in-line fourth quarter and full-year 2025, with comparable revenue up 3% in the quarter and 2% for the year at constant currencies and on the current structure, supported by growth in Network Infrastructure and Mobile Networks. Comparable gross margin improved in Q4 to 48.1% on a favorable product mix, though comparable operating margin declined to 17.3% due to growth investments in Network Infrastructure and the integration of Infinera, and reported margins were weighed down by higher restructuring costs. Full-year comparable operating profit was €2.0 billion, free cash flow reached €1.5 billion, and Nokia ended the year with €3.4 billion in net cash; the board will seek authorization for a €0.14 per share distribution and the company guides comparable operating profit of €2.0–2.5 billion for 2026. Operationally, Nokia highlighted strong momentum in Network Infrastructure, particularly in optical and IP networks on AI and cloud customer demand, stable performance in mobile networks with a focus on profitability, and ongoing strength in its Technology licensing business. Strategically, the group is simplifying its operating model into two main segments—Network Infrastructure and Mobile Infrastructure—effective 2026, integrating core software, radio and standards activities under Mobile Infrastructure, and reinforcing its push into AI-native and future 6G networks, including through the Infinera acquisition and a new partnership with NVIDIA, positioning the company to capture long-term growth as AI drives higher network performance and reliability requirements.

More about Nokia

Nokia is a global telecommunications equipment and technology company that develops network infrastructure, mobile network solutions and related software and services for operators, cloud providers and enterprises. Its portfolio spans fixed and mobile broadband, IP and optical networks and technology licensing, with a growing strategic focus on AI-native networks, cloud-connected infrastructure and future 5G and 6G capabilities for data center, telecom and enterprise customers.

See more insights into 0HAF stock on TipRanks’ Stock Analysis page.

data.title: Bucher Industries Grows Orders but Sees 2025 Sales Decline Amid Uneven Market Recovery
data.description:
Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Bucher Industries AG ( (CH:BUCN) ) has provided an update.

Bucher Industries reported a 4.6% increase in group order intake to CHF 2.88 billion in 2025 as market conditions stabilised, particularly in Europe, despite continued economic policy and trade-tariff uncertainties; however, net sales fell 7.6% to CHF 2.91 billion, reflecting a low starting order book and weaker demand in certain segments, notably glass-forming machinery. The company expanded cost-saving measures and reduced headcount, especially in the US, while benefiting from a CHF 43 million gain on the sale of a non-operational property and targeting an underlying operating margin of around 8% for 2025 and 2026; divisions showed mixed performance, with Kuhn Group and Bucher Hydraulics driving order growth, Bucher Municipal holding sales at high levels with margin improvement, Emhart Glass facing significantly lower orders and profitability, and a share buyback programme progressing toward its 4% capital target, underscoring management’s focus on cash generation and shareholder returns amid a subdued but improving demand environment.

The most recent analyst rating on (CH:BUCN) stock is a Hold with a CHF380.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Bucher Industries AG stock, see the CH:BUCN Stock Forecast page.

More about Bucher Industries AG

Bucher Industries AG is a diversified Swiss industrial group operating globally in agricultural machinery (Kuhn Group), municipal vehicles (Bucher Municipal), hydraulic systems (Bucher Hydraulics), glass-forming equipment (Bucher Emhart Glass) and various niche engineering businesses under Bucher Specials. Its products and services serve core infrastructure, agriculture, construction and packaging markets, with a strong presence in Europe, North America and selected emerging markets.

Average Trading Volume: 14,567

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: CHF3.65B

data.title: Sanki Engineering Revamps Top Management, Appoints Nagoya as New President
data.description:
Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Sanki Engineering Co ( (JP:1961) ) has provided an announcement.

Sanki Engineering Co., Ltd. has announced a leadership reshuffle effective April 1, 2026, as part of efforts to renew its management structure and further increase the Group’s corporate value. Current Representative Director and President Hirokazu Ishida will become Representative Director and Chairman of the Board, while Director and Managing Executive Officer Kazuhiro Nagoya, who joined Sanki in 2024 after a long career at Mitsui & Co. in industrial machinery, aviation, and mobility businesses, will be promoted to Representative Director and President, and current Chairman Tsutomu Hasegawa will move to the role of Director. The appointment of Nagoya, with his broad experience in industrial and DX-related roles, signals a strategic emphasis on strengthening governance and corporate management capabilities, which may influence Sanki’s future operational direction and competitiveness.

More about Sanki Engineering Co

Sanki Engineering Co., Ltd., listed on the Prime Market of the Tokyo Stock Exchange (securities code 1961), operates in the engineering and construction-related services sector, providing technical solutions through a group structure aimed at enhancing overall corporate value.

data.description:
Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The latest update is out from Bastion Minerals Ltd ( (AU:BMO) ).

Vita Resources NL has formally changed its name from Bastion Minerals and will trade under a new ASX ticker, VTA, reflecting a strategic repositioning of the business under a refreshed management team. The company is broadening its asset base with the addition of early-stage Western Australian gold exploration projects, including the recently acquired Ninnis Gold Project, while continuing to advance its ICE Copper-Gold Project in Canada, where it has defined a JORC mineral resource of 6.43 Mt at 1.07% copper, and assessing further potential across its rare earths and copper assets in Sweden and Chile, signalling an active growth and diversification phase for shareholders.

The most recent analyst rating on (AU:BMO) stock is a Sell with a A$0.01 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Bastion Minerals Ltd stock, see the AU:BMO Stock Forecast page.

More about Bastion Minerals Ltd

Vita Resources NL, formerly Bastion Minerals, is an ASX-listed mineral exploration company with a diversified portfolio spanning early-stage gold projects in Western Australia, the ICE Copper-Gold Project in Canada, the Gyttorp rare earths project in Sweden and the Cometa copper project in Chile, positioning it within the global precious and base metals exploration sector.
